title: Salty Chewy Spanish Potatoes Pancakes
description: A kidfriendly, Whole30friendly Spanish pancake made with mashed potatoes, eggs, spring onions, and a gentle knead for a chewy texture.
instructions: Preheat a large non‑stick skillet over medium heat and lightly oil the surface with 1 tablespoon of olive oil.
While the pan heats, peel and cube the potatoes. Steam or boil them until tender, about 10–12 minutes. Drain and mash until smooth, leaving a few lumps for texture.
In a mixing bowl, whisk the eggs lightly. Add the mashed potatoes, spring onions, minced garlic, sea salt, pepper, potato starch (if using), and smoked paprika. Mix until combined.
Transfer the mixture to the warmed skillet, spreading it evenly. Drizzle the remaining tablespoon of olive oil around the edges.
Cook for 5–7 minutes, then flip the pancake gently using a large spatula. Cook the other side for another 5–7 minutes until golden brown and firm.
Slide the pancake onto a cutting board and let it rest for 1 minute. Slice into wedges, sprinkle with fresh parsley if desired, and serve warm.
Serve immediately. The pancakes are best enjoyed fresh but can be reheated gently in a skillet or oven.
